question.text: If Rani needs a corpus of Rs.3,00,48,832 at retirement in 25 years and can earn 12% return on investments, what is her approximate required monthly savings?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Using PMT(12%/12, 300, 0, -30048832, 1) where rate = 1% per month, nper = 300 months (25 years). The result is approximately Rs.15,835 per month. **The Trap:** Candidates may confuse the 25-year savings period (300 months) with the 20-year retirement period (240 months). The PMT uses the accumulation period. **Cross-Exam Context:** The PMT function is a core financial planning tool. Note the difference between beginning-of-period (type=1) and end-of-period (type=0) payments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Show clients how even small changes in the savings start date dramatically affect the monthly amount needed.
